commands.review_summary:
The PR updates Alibaba Token Plan usage fetching to try `/tool/user/info.json` for `sec_token`, map expired-token payloads to `loginRequired`, and cover both paths with focused tests.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main lacks the user-info SEC-token fallback and does not classify the shown expired-token payload as `loginRequired`; the PR’s focused stub tests encode both failing paths.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No code repair is identified; the contributor needs to add real behavior proof that automation cannot supply for their Alibaba session.

target.title: Fix Alibaba token plan SEC token fallback
target.body:
## Summary

- Add a user info JSON fallback for resolving Alibaba Token Plan `sec_token`
- Treat `PostonlyOrTokenError` / expired token payloads as `loginRequired`
- Cover the new SEC token fallback and token-expired error mapping in tests

## Why

Alibaba Token Plan requests can fail when the dashboard HTML preflight does not expose `sec_token`, even though the authenticated user info endpoint can still return it.

The API can also return expired-token payloads as `successResponse: false`. Those should prompt login/token refresh instead of surfacing as a generic API error.

## Proof

Before this change, a cookie-authenticated Token Plan request could continue without `sec_token` when the dashboard HTML preflight did not contain the token. In that state, the subscription summary API could return an expired-token payload:

```json
{
  "code": "PostonlyOrTokenError",
  "message": "Your request has expired. Please refresh the page.",
  "successResponse": false
}
```

This change adds a second preflight to `/tool/user/info.json` and uses `data.secToken` when present.

The focused test `SEC token preflight falls back to user info` verifies that:

- dashboard HTML returns no token
- user info returns `secToken`
- the final POST includes `sec_token=user-info-token`

The parser test `post only token payload maps to login required` verifies that expired-token responses now map to `loginRequired`.

## Validation

- `swift test --filter AlibabaTokenPlan`
- `swiftlint --strict --no-cache`
target.author: yanxinxue
